name: esm
tagline: Reference skill for EvolutionaryScale's `esm` Python SDK: protein generation, structure prediction, embeddings, and Forge/Biohub cloud infer
verdict: Clean, well-scoped SDK guide. Auth reads ESM_API_KEY from env only, explicitly warns against hardcoding/committing tokens, and pins the PyPI install to a fixed version. No shell-piped code, no hidden instructions, no reviewer manipulation. Minor nits: no stated negative trigger, mixed EvolutionaryScale/Biohub branding could confuse installers, and a shortene

highlights.summary: Community link uses a shortened bit.ly URL (line 326: https://bit.ly/3FKwcWd) whose destination is obscured; low severity since it's a doc link, not executable content.

Install source is PyPI with a pinned version (esm==3.2.3, line 246) which is good, but the GitHub link (line 319) points to 'Biohub/esm' while installation text attributes the package to 'Evolutionary
No explicit 'do not use when...' guidance anywhere in the frontmatter or body.
